generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT-based occupancy sensor deployed in physical building to capture real-time user presence data. Part of BOREALIS post-occupancy sensor network."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance TemperatureHumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor capturing real-time environmental metrics including temperature and humidity levels. Integrated via Eclipse Hono/Ditto middleware."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalDataTransmitted
]

instance LightSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT light sensor measuring ambient lighting conditions in building zones. Data used for TwinLight lighting control optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightingDataTransmitted
]

instance PostOccupancySensorNetwork : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "BOREALIS-compliant sensor network providing real-time feedback on environmental conditions, user presence, and system performance across building zones."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PostOccupancyDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time occupancy status data streamed from IoT sensors to DT via Eclipse Hono/Ditto protocol."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EnvironmentalDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Temperature and humidity data transmitted in real-time from IoT sensors using Eclipse Hono/Ditto configuration."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureHumiditySensor
]

instance LightingDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Ambient lighting measurements transmitted from IoT sensors to DT for TwinLight control optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightSensor
]

instance PostOccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Aggregated sensor data from BOREALIS post-occupancy network containing environmental and occupancy metrics."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PostOccupancySensorNetwork
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BimEnrichedSemanticModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "BIM-based semantic model enriched with device metadata (tenant ID, endpoint addresses, control logic) serving as foundational backbone for DT deployment."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HonoDittoConfigurator, ModelToModelTransformer
]

instance RuntimeDataModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Automatically generated runtime model via model-to-model and model-to-text transformations from BIM, enabling DT artifact deployment."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HonoDittoConfigurator
]

instance RealTimeSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated real-time sensor data (occupancy, environmental metrics, lighting) ingested via Eclipse Hono/Ditto for processing and visualization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted, EnvironmentalDataTransmitted, LightingDataTransmitted, PostOccupancyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngine5Visualizer, ReinforcementLearningOptimizer
]

instance DeviceMetadata : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Extracted device metadata (tenant ID, device ID, endpoint addresses) from BIM models used for automated sensor integration."
    DTDFVocab:fromData PostOccupancyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HonoDittoConfigurator
]

instance ControlPolicySemantics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Domain-specific control semantics (e.g., lighting control rules) embedded in BIM models for TwinLight framework execution."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TwinLightControlFramework
]

// C11: Enablers
instance HonoDittoConfigurator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated configurator that scans BIM models to extract device metadata and generate runtime interfaces for Eclipse Hono/Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, AutomatedControlConfigurationService
]

instance ModelToModelTransformer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-driven engineering framework enabling automated transformation of BIM models into deployable DT runtime artifacts."
    DTDFVocab:enables RuntimeDataModelGenerationService
]

instance UnrealEngine5Visualizer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D visualization engine providing immersive monitoring and interactive diagnostics of building operations through DT."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeVisualizationService, SimulationService
]

instance ReinforcementLearningOptimizer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "BOREALIS reinforcement learning framework for dynamic optimization of lighting systems based on real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveLightingOptimizationService
]

instance TwinLightControlFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "BIM-centric control framework embedding domain-specific lighting control logic and automating deployment to physical systems."
    DTDFVocab:enables AutomatedLightingControlService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing continuous monitoring of building operations through real-time sensor data ingestion and processing."
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingStateInsight, OccupancyPatternInsight
]

instance PredictiveLightingOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service using BOREALIS reinforcement learning to optimize lighting for energy efficiency, visual comfort, and health effects."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyRecommendation, VisualComfortRecommendation
]

instance AutomatedControlConfigurationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service automating deployment of control policies from BIM-enriched models to physical building systems."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlPolicyDeploymentAction
]

instance RealTimeVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing immersive 3D visualization of building operations through Unreal Engine 5 for diagnostics and control."
    DTDFVocab:provides InteractiveBuildingVisualizationInsight
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling simulation of control scenarios before physical implementation using DT visualization."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlScenarioSimulationInsight
]

instance RuntimeDataModelGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service generating deployable runtime models from BIM via model-to-model transformations."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeployableRuntimeModelInsight
]

instance AutomatedLightingControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service automating lighting control based on BIM-enriched control policies and real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyRecommendation, VisualComfortRecommendation
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance BuildingStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time insight into current building operational state derived from aggregated sensor data."
]

instance OccupancyPatternInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analyzed patterns of user occupancy across building zones enabling optimized resource allocation."
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated recommendation to adjust lighting systems for maximum energy efficiency based on occupancy and environmental data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance VisualComfortRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recommendation to optimize lighting for visual comfort and non-visual health effects using BOREALIS algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlPolicyDeploymentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated deployment of control policies from BIM models to physical building systems via TwinLight framework."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance InteractiveBuildingVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D visualization of building operations enabling facility managers to interact with the DT for diagnostics."
]

instance ControlScenarioSimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulated results of potential control scenarios before physical implementation using DT visualization."
]

instance DeployableRuntimeModelInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Generated runtime model ready for deployment to physical building systems from BIM transformations."
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building control system actuator for lighting systems configured via TwinLight's BIM-centric control framework."
]

instance HvacController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "PLC-based HVAC controller executing control logic derived from BIM-enriched models for dynamic environmental control."
]

instance IoTGateway : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT gateway enabling bidirectional communication between DT and physical building systems via Eclipse Hono/Ditto."
]

// C1: System Under Study
instance AeCoBuildingInfrastructureDigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:DigitalTwin [
    base:desc "Digital Twin system representing AECO domain building infrastructures (academic/commercial facilities) with lifecycle support from design to operations."
    base:contains BimEnrichedSemanticModel, RuntimeDataModel, RealTimeSensorData, DeviceMetadata, ControlPolicySemantics
    DTDFVocab:uses HonoDittoConfigurator, ModelToModelTransformer, UnrealEngine5Visualizer, ReinforcementLearningOptimizer, TwinLightControlFramework
    base:isContainedIn PhysicalBuildingEnvironment
]

instance PhysicalBuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains LightingActuator, HvacController, IoTGateway
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The interactions from the virtual world to the physical world are enabled through:
  - **Bidirectional control commands** generated by the DT and transmitted via **Eclipse Hono/Ditto** to physical actuators (e.g., lighting systems, HVAC controls). These commands are derived from real-time analytics, optimization algorithms (e.g., reinforcement learning in **BOREALIS**), or user-defined control logic embedded in the BIM model.
  - **Automated commissioning** via **TwinLight’s framework**, which translates BIM-enriched control semantics into executable runtime configurations for physical devices. This ensures that virtual adjustments (e.g., energy-saving policies) are directly applied to the physical infrastructure.
  - **Service-oriented integration points** in the DT architecture, allowing custom services (e.g., predictive maintenance, adaptive control) to generate and dispatch actuation commands to the physical twin."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale is **real-time to near-real-time**, with synchronization frequencies determined by:
  - **Sensor data acquisition rates** (e.g., environmental sensors may update every 1–5 seconds, while occupancy sensors may update less frequently).
  - **Control loop latency** (e.g., adaptive lighting systems in **BOREALIS** may adjust every few minutes based on reinforcement learning iterations).
  - **Middleware performance** (e.g., **Eclipse Hono/Ditto** supports millisecond-level latency for critical IoT communications).
  The DT is designed to maintain **continuous synchronization** with the physical twin, with the **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric** used to evaluate and ensure high fidelity between virtual and physical states."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is orchestrated as a **service-oriented architecture** with the following components:
  - **Central DT Framework**: Acts as the hub for bidirectional data exchange, integrating BIM models, runtime data, and user services.
  - **Middleware Layer (Hono/Ditto)**: Handles real-time communication between physical and virtual systems, ensuring seamless data synchronization.
  - **Visualization Layer (Unreal Engine 5)**: Provides immersive interfaces for facility managers to interact with the DT.
  - **Extension Points**: Standardized interfaces for integrating custom services (e.g., energy optimization, predictive maintenance) developed by **DT Engineers**.
  - **BIM Integration Layer**: Transforms static BIM models into dynamic DT artifacts via automated model transformations, ensuring semantic coherence between design and operation."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT covers the following lifecycle phases of its physical counterpart:
  - **Design phase**: Leverages **BIM models** as the foundational semantic representation, enriched with device metadata and control logic for initial deployment.
  - **Construction phase**: Supports clash detection, material tracking, and automated handover of operational data to the DT.
  - **Operational phase**: Enables real-time monitoring, predictive maintenance, and adaptive control (e.g., via **BOREALIS’s reinforcement learning**) throughout the building’s lifecycle.
  - **Refurbishment/Adaptation phase**: Facilitates dynamic updates to the DT by integrating new sensor/actuator data and evolving control policies without requiring a full redeployment. The DT’s modular architecture ensures compatibility with future upgrades."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**: Quantifies the alignment between the DT and its physical counterpart, validating the DT’s accuracy and responsiveness.
  - **Automated validation of BIM models**: Ensures that enriched BIM models (e.g., with device metadata) are syntactically and semantically correct before deployment.
  - **Real-time data synchronization**: **Eclipse Hono/Ditto** guarantees low-latency communication, minimizing discrepancies between virtual and physical states.
  - **Reinforcement learning feedback loops (BOREALIS)**: Continuously refine control policies based on real-time sensor data, reducing uncertainty and improving predictive accuracy.
  - **Modular architecture**: Allows isolated updates to services or components without disrupting the entire DT, ensuring incremental validation and evolution."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical twin (PT) and DT includes:
  - **Network protocols**: **Eclipse Hono** uses lightweight M2M protocols (e.g., MQTT, AMQP) for efficient IoT communication, while **Ditto** provides standardized digital twin interfaces.
  - **Middleware architecture**: A **service-oriented middleware layer** (Hono/Ditto) abstracts the heterogeneity of physical devices, enabling seamless integration of sensors/actuators with the DT.
  - **Endpoint addressing**: Device metadata (e.g., tenant ID, endpoint addresses) extracted from BIM models ensures that physical components are uniquely identified and accessible for bidirectional communication.
  - **Data normalization**: Sensor data is normalized and validated before ingestion into the DT to ensure consistency and interoperability."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is hosted and deployed using:
  - **Cloud-native or edge computing**: The **Eclipse Hono/Ditto** framework supports scalable deployment, with components potentially distributed across cloud and edge environments for low-latency processing.
  - **Modular microservices**: The DT’s architecture allows individual services (e.g., visualization, optimization) to be deployed independently, enabling flexible scaling and updates.
  - **BIM-driven automation**: The deployment process is automated via **model-to-model and model-to-text transformations**, reducing manual intervention and ensuring reproducibility.
  - **Unreal Engine 5 integration**: For visualization, which can be hosted on dedicated rendering servers or integrated into facility management dashboards."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration is achieved through:
  - **Standardized middleware interfaces (Hono/Ditto)**: Enable interoperability with external systems (e.g., ERP, CMMS, or other DTs) via open protocols (e.g., MQTT, AMQP).
  - **BIM as a semantic backbone**: Facilitates data exchange with other AECO tools (e.g., CAD, facility management software) by leveraging shared data schemas and metadata.
  - **Service-oriented architecture**: Allows the DT to expose APIs for integration with third-party systems, enabling seamless data flow across organizational boundaries."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT adheres to the following standards and frameworks:
  - **Building Information Modeling (BIM)**: As the semantic backbone for representing building geometry, topology, and component relationships.
  - **Eclipse Hono/Ditto**: For standardized IoT and digital twin interfaces, ensuring interoperability with other middleware solutions.
  - **Model-driven engineering (MDE)**: Principles for automating the transformation of BIM models into deployable DT artifacts, reducing manual effort and ensuring consistency.
  - **Industry-specific standards**: While not explicitly named, the framework is designed to align with AECO domain standards (e.g., IFC for BIM interoperability) and IoT protocols (e.g., MQTT, AMQP) for real-time communication."
]
